auth.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
auth.forgotPasswordDesc: Enter your email address and we'll send you a reset link.
auth.resetEmailSent: If that email is registered, we've sent a reset link. Check your inbox (and spam folder).
auth.passwordResetSuccess: Password updated. You can now log in.

dashboard.title: Your projects
dashboard.tier1Desc: 3–5 minutes. Job title + bullets → 2 variants → download.
dashboard.tier2Desc: 30–60 minutes. Full workflow → job analysis, posting, candidate profile, interview guide.
dashboard.noProjects: No projects yet. Create your first job posting.
dashboard.trashNote: Deleted projects are permanently removed after 7 days.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.network: Connection error. Please check your internet connection.
errors.unauthorized: You need to log in to access this page.
errors.forbidden: You do not have access to this resource.
errors.boundary: Something went wrong on this page.

tier2.step1Sub: Upload your existing job posting template so the AI matches your tone and structure. Skip this step if you don't have one.
tier2.step1Drop: Drag a .docx or .pdf file here, or click to select
tier2.step2Sub: Responsibilities, details and recruitment context. The AI uses all fields as the basis for fit criteria and candidate profile.
tier2.needDateLabel: When does the role need to be filled?
tier2.departmentPlaceholder: e.g. Growth team, Marketing, Backend Engineering
tier2.teamCompositionPlaceholder: e.g. 6-person team: 3 engineers, 1 designer, 1 PM, 1 data analyst — close collaboration with product
tier2.step3Sub: The AI suggests 4 fit categories based on the role and team. Edit them or use them as-is.
tier2.step3WithSuggestion: Edit the suggestion, or write your own below
tier2.fitJobDesc: What the candidate needs to master professionally to perform the job
tier2.fitTeamDesc: What is required to collaborate effectively with this specific team
tier2.fitLeaderDesc: What is required in the relationship with the direct manager and leadership structure
tier2.fitCultureDesc: Behavioural and work patterns that fit this environment
tier2.step4Sub: The profile you score candidates against. Think in 3 dimensions: experience/skills, previous environment, ways of working.
tier2.step4InfoBox: The difference from responsibilities: responsibilities describe the role in the job posting. The candidate profile is an internal assessment tool for the interview.
tier2.dim1: Experience and skills — what they have done and know
tier2.dim2: Previous environment — what type of organisation fits
tier2.dim3: Working style — how they work best
tier2.step5Sub: 3 questions that help the AI generate a more precise and evidence-based recruitment foundation.
tier2.step5Q1Sub: Think of the best person you've seen in a similar role. What did they specifically do day-to-day? Describe actions — not traits.
tier2.step5Q1Placeholder: e.g. "Set up weekly 1-1s with all 7 team members, always starting by asking about blockers — not status updates"
tier2.step5Q2Sub: What did the person who failed in this type of role specifically do wrong? Describe specific actions or patterns.
tier2.step5Q2Placeholder: e.g. "Focused on processes rather than relationships, lost insight into what teams were actually working on"
tier2.step5Q3Sub: What is the requirement that isn't written in the job posting, but determines whether someone succeeds in the role?
tier2.step5Q3Placeholder: e.g. "The ability to navigate internal politics and build buy-in with leadership without formal authority"

completeness.allClearSub: No missing elements found in your input.
completeness.subtitleSingle: One element is often missing from job postings — add details or generate anyway.
completeness.subtitlePlural: {{n}} elements are often missing from job postings — add details or generate anyway.

tier1.introSub: Write the job title and 5–10 bullets about what the role actually involves. We challenge you where the research says otherwise.
tier1.jobTitleSub: The title candidates see at the top of the posting.
tier1.bulletsSub: Concrete responsibilities, requirements and context. One bullet per line.
tier1.bulletsHint: 5–6 bullets describing key responsibilities and what you're looking for
tier1.detailsSub: Fill in what you know. The rest can be added later.
tier1.departmentPlaceholder: e.g. Growth team, Marketing, Backend Engineering
tier1.teamCompositionPlaceholder: e.g. 6-person team: 3 engineers, 1 designer, 1 PM — works closely with product
tier1.templateHint: Upload a .docx so the posting matches your tone and layout.
tier1.templateDrop: Drag a file here, or browse
tier1.generatingSub: Running bias checks · Calling Claude AI · Usually 10–20 seconds
tier1.refusedBody: This content cannot be generated as it violates our guidelines.
tier1.refusedHint: Try rephrasing your job title and bullets.
tier1.mixNote: Pick the best sections from both variants and combine them
tier1.wsEmpty: Click a section to add it
tier1.editHint: You can edit the text below before downloading.
tier1.downloadHint: Saves as a Word document and marks the project as completed

dashboard.title: Your projects
dashboard.tier1Desc: Describe the role in short bullets. We challenge clichés and biases in real time and generate a complete posting in Danish or English.
dashboard.tier2Desc: Includes competency profile, screening criteria and interview guide — everything you need for a full recruitment process.
dashboard.emptyAll: No projects yet — create your first one above.
dashboard.confirmDelete:
Delete project "{{name}}"?

It will be moved to trash and permanently deleted after 7 days.
dashboard.tier1Desc2: 3–5 minutes. Job title + bullets → 2 variants → download.
dashboard.tier2Desc2: 30–60 minutes. Full workflow → job analysis, posting, candidate profile, interview guide.
dashboard.noProjects: No projects yet. Create your first job posting.
dashboard.trashNote: Deleted projects are permanently removed after 7 days.
